channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea says its recent missile tests involved new ballistic missile with 'super-large warhead'
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Tuesday it had test-fired a new tactical ballistic missile capable of carrying a huge warhead, as the country is pushing to modernize its weapons arsenal to cope with what it calls U.S.-led threats.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tourist plane crashes onto highway in France, killing three: video
item.description: French authorities are investigating a plane crash that took the lives of at least three people. A tourist plane crashed on the side of the A4 highway near Collegien.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ahead of election, Venezuela's Maduro says he has agreed to resume negotiations with United States
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s government plans to resume negotiations with the U.S. government this week, President Nicolás Maduro announced Monday, less than a month before a highly anticipated election in which he and his party are facing their toughest challenge in decades.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Beryl razes southeast Caribbean as a record-breaking Category 4 storm
item.description: BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (AP) — Hurricane Beryl ripped off doors, windows and roofs in homes across the southeastern Caribbean on Monday after making landfall on the island of Carriacou as the earliest storm of Category 4 strength to form in the Atlantic, fueled by record warm waters.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO and KOFI JONES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Albania's world-renowned novelist Ismail Kadare dies at 88
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albanian novelist and poet Ismail Kadare, whose irreverent works from inside communist Albania earned him international renown and repression from the country's dictatorship, has died in Tirana, his publishing editor said Monday. He was 88.
item.dc:creator: VLASOV SULAJ and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged Sinaloa cartel financial operator extradited to US
item.description: An alleged Sinaloa cartel “money man” will face American justice now that Mexico has extradited him to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: Stolen at birth, an adoptee sues Chile over thousands of similar dictatorship-era crimes
item.description: SANTIAGO, Chile (AP) — A Chilean-American raised in the United States filed a criminal complaint against the Chilean government on Monday, alleging that it engaged in a systematic plan to steal thousands of babies from perceived enemies of the state in the 1970s and 1980s.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's right-wing National Rally looks to seize on recent electoral gains
item.description: The right-wing, Marine Le Pen-led National Rally is closer than ever to control of France's government, and looking to make the most out of its first-round electoral dominance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN denies recognition of Taliban government following multinational meeting
item.description: A U.N.-led, Afghanistan-focused meeting with the Taliban does not translate to recognition of the group's claim to the government in Kabul, an official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel destroys Islamic Jihad’s largest rocket production site in Gaza
item.description: Israeli forces destroyed the largest rocket manufacturing site used by the Palestinian Islamic Jihad as terrorists fire strikes from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel orders Palestinians to flee Khan Younis, signaling likely new assault on southern Gaza city
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli army ordered a mass evacuation of Palestinians from much of Khan Younis on Monday, a sign that troops are likely to launch a new ground assault into the Gaza Strip’s second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: José Raúl Mulino sworn in as Panama's new president, promises to stop migration through Darien Gap
item.description: José Raúl Mulino has been sworn in as Panama’s next president, facing pressure to slow migration through the Darien Gap that connects his country with Colombia.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine thwarts plot to overthrow government in failed coup attempt
item.description: An alleged plot to overthrow the Ukrainian government by forcibly ousting military and civilian leadership in Kyiv was stopped by security officials following a months-long investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Organization announces new project in Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Trump Organization will construct a Trump Tower building in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: ADL sues Iran, North Korea and Syria over Hamas's Oct. 7 attack
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League (ADL) is going to court seeking to hold Iran, North Korea and Syria responsible for its role in aiding Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Israeli plane refused fuel in Turkey after emergency landing: Report
item.description: Turkish officials have denied the claims, saying fuel was to be provided due to "humanitarian considerations.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: McLaughlin-Levrone sends pre-Olympic jolt, runs 50.65 to break world record (again) in 400 hurdles
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — For the better part of two years, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone all but disappeared from the 400-meter hurdles course. Turns out, she wasn't hiding or looking for something else to do. Just getting better at what she does best.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Firefighters tackle blazes on Greek islands of Chios and Kos as premier warns of 'dangerous summer'
item.description: Firefighters battled wildfires that broke out on the eastern Aegean islands of Chios and Kos and injured five people, as Greece’s prime minister warned of a dangerous summer ahead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ultra-Orthodox protest against order to enlist in Israeli military turns violent in Jerusalem
item.description: Israeli police said protesters threw rocks and attacked the car of an ultra-Orthodox Cabinet minister.
item.dc:creator: OHAD ZWIGENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea test-launches 2 ballistic missiles, after end of new US-South Korea-Japan drill
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea test-fired two ballistic missile Monday but one of them possibly flew abnormally, South Korea's military said, a day after the North vowed “offensive and overwhelming” responses to a new U.S. military drill with South Korea and Japan.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French far right scores convincing first-round election win: What to know
item.description: The far-right National Rally party in France scored a convincing early lead in the first round of the country’s parliamentary elections Sunday, according to pollsters, bringing controversial leader Marine Le Pen a step closer to forming a government.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: French voters propel far-right National Rally to strong lead in first-round legislative elections
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The far-right National Rally leaped into a strong lead Sunday in France's first round of legislative elections, polling agencies projected, bringing the party closer to being able to form a government in round two and dealing a major slap to centrist President Emmanuel Macron and his ri
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, LORI HINNANT AND SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Storms in Switzerland and Italy cause flooding and landslides, leaving at least 4 people dead
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Storms in Switzerland and northern Italy caused extensive flooding and landslides, leaving at least four people dead, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US struggles to deter Houthi threat as crisis spirals
item.description: After half a year of conflict, the U.S. has failed to deter the Houthi rebels from attacking merchant ships in the Red Sea as the Yemeni fighters continue to sink commercial boats and disrupt global trade, posing an increasingly difficult challenge for the far larger American military. Repeated U.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Most of the world prefers Biden but is preparing for Trump
item.description: Former President Trump’s apparent victory in the presidential debate Thursday night has added urgency to efforts underway by world leaders to prepare for a second Trump administration, despite international audiences preferring President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Twice-yearly injection fully protects women from HIV, trial finds
item.description: The trial showed the new injection "demonstrated 100% efficacy as a preventive treatment" for HIV among African women.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC
